name: metrics-dashboard
description: Exercises the markup chart element and icon-in-button: a latency dashboard with a real chart pane, icon-carrying buttons, and deterministic seeded data.
prompt:
This workspace is a freshly scaffolded Native SDK native-frontend app (see README.md; the view is src/app.native, the logic src/main.zig). Replace the counter with a small service metrics dashboard.

Requirements:
- A header row with the app title exactly "Service Metrics" and two buttons labeled exactly "Refresh" and "Reset". Each button shows a small vector glyph inside the button itself, before its label — one control per button (a single hit target whose glyph and label enable/disable together), never a separate image stacked next to a button.
- The dashboard owns a latency history: exactly 24 seeded samples in milliseconds, deterministic (computed or literal in the model — no randomness, no clock). The most recent sample must be exactly 42.
- The history renders as a real drawn chart: a line or bar plot of the series inside a panel, produced by the framework's chart element — not rows of text, not per-sample rectangles hand-assembled from generic widgets. The chart must appear in the accessibility/automation tree as a chart. Pin the y-axis floor at zero.
- Pressing "Refresh" appends the next sample and drops the oldest, keeping exactly 24: the appended value is (previous latest sample + 7) modulo 120, plus 20. Pressing "Reset" restores the original seeded series. The chart and the status line update live.
- A status bar with the exact text "24 samples · last <n> ms" (so "24 samples · last 42 ms" at first), updating live.
- Keep the model and all update logic in src/main.zig. Author the whole view — header, chart, status bar, page structure — in markup (src/app.native).
- Validate the markup with `native markup check` on your .native files, and keep `zig build test` green — extend src/tests.zig so the tests cover Refresh (window slides, exact appended value) and Reset through the real dispatch path.

When you are done, make sure `zig build test` passes and summarize what you built.

checks.description: the plot is the framework chart element (<chart> with <series> bindings)
buttons carry their glyph inline via icon=
the chart pins its baseline with y-min="0"
live snapshot shows the chart role, both icon buttons, the title, and the seeded summary

criteria: The chart is composed correctly: a <chart> element whose <series> binds a model f32 iterable, with token colors and a pinned zero baseline — no hand-rolled bar stacks, no raw colors, no Zig-root inversion just for the chart.
The view is markup-first: the whole page (header, buttons, chart, status bar) lives in src/app.native, and the Zig side owns only the model, messages, and update logic.

Model/Msg/update follow The Elm Architecture idiomatically: the sample window has one source of truth, the appended-value rule and the summary string are model logic, Refresh/Reset are distinct messages, nothing derivable is stored.
Tests are meaningful: Refresh asserts the slide and the exact appended value, Reset restores the seed, both through the real dispatch path.
checks.description: quality of the chart markup and model logic beyond compliance
